body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Reasoning

**Agent supports (yes).** Pi's REPL lets the user type a follow-up message WHILE a turn is in flight and queues it: Enter steers/queues the buffered text, Alt+Enter inserts a newline. The queued text is not dropped — it is consumed after the current turn settles. This satisfies the scenario's essence (user sends a follow-up mid-turn; the runtime buffers it without dropping it).

**Irrlicht observes (partial).** The pi adapter is `FilesUnderRoot` over `~/.pi/agent/sessions/**.jsonl`, and the parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go`) only flips the session back to `ready` on a `turn_done` event — i.e. a `{"type":"message","message":{"role":"assistant","stopReason":"stop"}}` line. A `user` line is classified as `user_message` and does NOT by itself emit a turn boundary or a ready transition. So a queued user line landing mid-turn cannot inject a working→ready flicker: the in-flight turn stays `working` until its own `stopReason:stop`.

The partial carve-out is in HOW pi consumes the queued message, which differs from claudecode/codex. claudecode/codex FOLD the queued follow-up into the SAME turn (one contiguous working span, one task boundary). Pi instead consumes the queued message as the NEXT turn after the current one settles: the in-flight turn completes (working→ready), then the queued message produces its OWN working→ready arc. So the observable is TWO arcs on one rollout session, not one contiguous span. The negative invariant the scenario cares about still holds at the transcript layer — the queued user line introduces no extra mid-turn state flip, no waiting, and the session ends at ready — but irrlicht cannot observe the queue as a single uninterrupted working span the way it does for claudecode. Hence `partial`, confidence 0.7.

## What proves each signal

- "State stays working when the queued message arrives; no →ready flicker": the first turn's working span is contiguous through the queued user line because only `stopReason:stop` flips to ready, and the queued `user` line is not a stop. Observable.
- "Cycle eventually ends at ready": final `turn_done` on the rollout session → ready; single `process_exited` at REPL teardown. Observable.
- "No turn boundary on queued arrival": the queued message is a `user_message`, which the parser does not treat as a turn boundary; the boundary is the in-flight turn's own `stop`. Observable, but pi then opens a SECOND turn for the queued message rather than folding it — this is the partial.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: pi consumes the queued follow-up as a SEPARATE next turn (two working→ready arcs on one session), not folded into the in-flight turn as claudecode/codex do (one contiguous span). The scenario's negative invariant (no mid-turn ready flicker, no waiting, ends ready) still holds, but the 'single contiguous working span' phrasing from the claudecode recipe does not apply to pi.

sources.note: Only stopReason=="stop" maps to turn_done; a user line is user_message and emits no turn boundary, so a queued user line cannot inject a working→ready flicker.
Source is FilesUnderRoot over ~/.pi/agent/sessions — append-only JSONL transcript the daemon tails for state classification.
pi REPL banner documents Enter (steer/queue) vs Alt+Enter (newline); a second send before the first wait_turn types the follow-up while the turn is in flight.
Cross-adapter contrast: codex folds the queued message into one task_complete; pi does not, hence the partial.
